content: I'm working on upstreaming the device bindings and I took notice that Device is used nowhere and that RawDevice only does printing. This made me think that perhaps Device should be dropped and RawDevice should be changed for DeviceOps, a trait for device operations like printing, adding files to sysfs, and so forth. Thoughts?

It was an old design. The new API design with asref is https://github.com/nbdd0121/linux/commit/e2f30596dbb8a0f590b4f35039800519c4f3d8c9 here although I haven't yet submitted it because there're no driver that needs this ATM

Gary Guo said:

It was an old design. The new API design with asref is https://github.com/nbdd0121/linux/commit/e2f30596dbb8a0f590b4f35039800519c4f3d8c9 here although I haven't yet submitted it because there're no driver that needs this ATM

So that's the new design with ARef, quite neat, and gives Device a purpose as representing a parent device, which may or may not be trivial to point its type out.
Because I'll try to upstream my work on USB I'm going to add your work on top of mine's if you don't mind.
Better yet, I've come to the realization that RawDevice is actually kinda pointless, due to the possibility to rather implement AsRef<Device> for your type.
If you actually have to incur to get a raw pointer to struct device it'd be better to have dedicated methods in your impls I think.
Please note that we give the chance to the original authors to upstream their work on their own unless they don't want to or can't. Of course, you can still base your work on top of theirs.
